examples.sentence: 彼はプロのボクサー並みの力を持っています。
examples.translation: He has power equivalent to a professional boxer.
examples.translationEn: He has power equivalent to a professional boxer.
examples.romaji: Kare wa puro no bokusaa nami no chikara o motte imasu.

examples.sentence: 彼女の料理の腕前はレストラン並みです。
examples.translation: Her cooking skills are like those of a restaurant.
examples.translationEn: Her cooking skills are like those of a restaurant.
examples.romaji: Kanojo no ryouri no udemae wa resutoran nami desu.

examples.sentence: このエリアの夏は砂漠並みの暑さです。
examples.translation: The summer heat in this area is like a desert.
examples.translationEn: The summer heat in this area is like a desert.
examples.romaji: Kono eria no natsu wa sabaku nami no atsusa desu.

examples.sentence: 今日の仕事は社長並みの忙しさだった。
examples.translation: Today's work was as busy as a president's.
examples.translationEn: Today's work was as busy as a president's.
examples.romaji: Kyou no shigoto wa shachou nami no isogashisa datta.

examples.sentence: 説明できる英語力がある前提で、この仕事を引き受けてください。
examples.translation: Please take on this job on the assumption that you have enough English skills to explain.
examples.translationEn: Please take on this job on the assumption that you have enough English skills to explain.
examples.romaji: Setsumei dekiru eigo-ryoku ga aru zentei de, kono shigoto o hikiukete kudasai.

examples.sentence: 彼が一緒に行く前提で、旅行の計画を立てました。
examples.translation: I planned the trip on the assumption that he would go together.
examples.translationEn: I planned the trip on the assumption that he would go together.
examples.romaji: Kare ga issho ni iku zentei de, ryokou no keikaku o tatemashita.

examples.sentence: 成功する前提で、このプロジェクトを進めています。
examples.translation: We are moving forward with this project on the assumption that we will succeed.
examples.translationEn: We are moving forward with this project on the assumption that we will succeed.
examples.romaji: Seikou suru zentei de, kono purojekuto o susumete imasu.

examples.sentence: 雨が降らない前提で、ピクニックの準備をしました。
examples.translation: I prepared for the picnic under the condition that it wouldn't rain.
examples.translationEn: I prepared for the picnic under the condition that it wouldn't rain.
examples.romaji: Ame ga furanai zentei de, pikunikku no junbi o shimashita.

examples.grammarPoint: Verbる / Noun(である) + 限り(は) (kagiri (wa))
examples.sentence: 私が生きている限りは、あなたを支えます。
examples.translation: As long as I'm alive, I will support you.
examples.translationEn: As long as I'm alive, I will support you.
examples.romaji: Watashi ga ikite iru kagiri wa, anata o sasaemasu.

examples.grammarPoint: Verbる / Noun(である) + 限り(は) (kagiri (wa))
examples.sentence: 子供が学校に通っている限り、私たちは彼らを守ります。
examples.translation: As long as the kids are going to school, we will protect them.
examples.translationEn: As long as the kids are going to school, we will protect them.
examples.romaji: Kodomo ga gakkou ni kayotte iru kagiri, watashitachi wa karera o mamorimasu.

examples.grammarPoint: Verbる / Noun(である) + 限り(は) (kagiri (wa))
examples.sentence: 能力がある限り、最善を尽くします。
examples.translation: I will do my best as long as I have the ability.
examples.translationEn: I will do my best as long as I have the ability.
examples.romaji: Nouryoku ga aru kagiri, saizen o tsukushimasu.

examples.grammarPoint: Verbる / Noun(である) + 限り(は) (kagiri (wa))
examples.sentence: あなたがここにいる限り、私は安心です。
examples.translation: As long as you're here, I feel safe.
examples.translationEn: As long as you're here, I feel safe.
examples.romaji: Anata ga koko ni iru kagiri, watashi wa anshin desu.

examples.sentence: 散歩がてらに郵便局へ行った。
examples.translation: I went to the post office while taking a walk.
examples.translationEn: I went to the post office while taking a walk.
examples.romaji: Sanpo gatera ni yūbinkyoku e itta.

examples.sentence: 買い物がてらに友達に会いに行った。
examples.translation: I went to see a friend while doing some shopping.
examples.translationEn: I went to see a friend while doing some shopping.
examples.romaji: Kaimono gatera ni tomodachi ni ai ni itta.

examples.sentence: ジョギングがてらに新聞を買いに行った。
examples.translation: I went to buy a newspaper while jogging.
examples.translationEn: I went to buy a newspaper while jogging.
examples.romaji: Jogingu gatera ni shinbun o kai ni itta.

examples.sentence: 公園でピクニックがてら読書をした。
examples.translation: I read a book while having a picnic in the park.
examples.translationEn: I read a book while having a picnic in the park.
examples.romaji: Kōen de pikunikku gatera dokusho o shita.

examples.sentence: 彼は友達を助けこそすれ、自分を卑下することはありません。
examples.translation: He might help his friends, but he never belittles himself.
examples.translationEn: He might help his friends, but he never belittles himself.
examples.romaji: Kare wa tomodachi o tasuke koso sure, jibun o higesuru koto wa arimasen.

examples.sentence: 彼女は料理こそすれ、掃除はほとんどしない。
examples.translation: She might cook, but she rarely cleans.
examples.translationEn: She might cook, but she rarely cleans.
examples.romaji: Kanojo wa ryōri koso sure, sōji wa hotondo shinai.

examples.sentence: 私の兄は勉強こそすれ、遊ぶ時間がほとんどない。
examples.translation: He might study a lot, but he barely has time to have fun.
examples.translationEn: He might study a lot, but he barely has time to have fun.
examples.romaji: Watashi no ani wa benkyō koso sure, asobu jikan ga hotondo nai.

examples.sentence: 母は働きこそすれ、自分のために時間を使うことはほとんどない。
examples.translation: My mother might work a lot, but she hardly spends any time on herself.
examples.translationEn: My mother might work a lot, but she hardly spends any time on herself.
examples.romaji: Haha wa hataraki koso sure, jibun no tame ni jikan o tsukau koto wa hotondo nai.

examples.sentence: 彼に無理やり飲ませられた。
examples.translation: He forced me to drink against my will.
examples.translationEn: He forced me to drink against my will.
examples.romaji: Kare ni muriyari nomaserareta.

examples.sentence: 私は父に勉強させられました。
examples.translation: My father made me study.
examples.translationEn: My father made me study.
examples.romaji: Watashi wa chichi ni benkyō saseraremashita.

examples.sentence: 子供たちは毎日ピアノを弾かせられる。
examples.translation: The kids are made to play the piano every day.
examples.translationEn: The kids are made to play the piano every day.
examples.romaji: Kodomo-tachi wa mainichi piano o hikaserareru.

examples.sentence: 私たちは強制的に早起きさせられました。
examples.translation: We were forcibly made to wake up early.
examples.translationEn: We were forcibly made to wake up early.
examples.romaji: Watashitachi wa kyōsei-teki ni hayaoki saseraremashita.

examples.sentence: この仕事が嫌ならもう辞めざるを得ない。
examples.translation: If you hate this job, you have no choice but to quit.
examples.translationEn: If you hate this job, you have no choice but to quit.
examples.romaji: Kono shigoto ga iya nara mō yamezaru o enai.

examples.sentence: 試験が近いので、夜遅くまで勉強せざるを得ない。
examples.translation: The exam is near, so I have no choice but to study late into the night.
examples.translationEn: The exam is near, so I have no choice but to study late into the night.
examples.romaji: Shiken ga chikai no de, yoru osoku made benkyō sezaru o enai.

examples.sentence: 涙が止まらない。泣かざるを得ない。
examples.translation: My tears won't stop. I can't help but cry.
examples.translationEn: My tears won't stop. I can't help but cry.
examples.romaji: Namida ga tomaranai. Nakazaru o enai.

examples.sentence: 先生の命令だから、従わざるを得ない。
examples.translation: It's the teacher's order, so I have no choice but to comply.
examples.translationEn: It's the teacher's order, so I have no choice but to comply.
examples.romaji: Sensei no meirei dakara, shitagawazaru o enai.

examples.sentence: 日本へ来たのに、一度も富士山を見ずじまいで帰国してしまった。
examples.translation: I came to Japan but ended up going home without ever getting to see Mt. Fuji.
examples.translationEn: I came to Japan but ended up going home without ever getting to see Mt. Fuji.
examples.romaji: Nihon e kita noni, ichidomo Fuji-san o mizu jimai de kikoku shite shimatta.

examples.sentence: 出張のため、彼女に会わずじまいだった。
examples.translation: Because I was on a business trip, I ended up not meeting her.
examples.translationEn: Because I was on a business trip, I ended up not meeting her.
examples.romaji: Shucchō no tame, kanojo ni awazu jimai datta.

examples.sentence: 昨日は疲れすぎて、宿題をせずじまいだ。
examples.translation: I was too tired yesterday and ended up not doing my homework.
examples.translationEn: I was too tired yesterday and ended up not doing my homework.
examples.romaji: Kinō wa tsukare-sugite, shukudai o sezu jimai da.

examples.sentence: 着物を買ったが、結局、一度も着ずじまいだ。
examples.translation: I bought a kimono, but in the end, I never got the chance to wear it.
examples.translationEn: I bought a kimono, but in the end, I never got the chance to wear it.
examples.romaji: Kimono o katta ga, kekkyoku, ichido mo kizu jimai da.

examples.sentence: 朝ごはんを食べずとも、働くことができます。
examples.translation: Even without eating breakfast, I can work.
examples.translationEn: Even without eating breakfast, I can work.
examples.romaji: Asagohan o tabezu tomo, hataraku koto ga dekimasu.

examples.sentence: 車がなくとも、バスで行くことができます。
examples.translation: Even without a car, you can go by bus.
examples.translationEn: Even without a car, you can go by bus.
examples.romaji: Kuruma ga nakutomo, basu de iku koto ga dekimasu.

examples.sentence: お金がなくとも、幸せになれる。
examples.translation: Even without money, one can be happy.
examples.translationEn: Even without money, one can be happy.
examples.romaji: Okane ga nakutomo, shiawase ni nareru.

examples.sentence: インターネットがなくとも、情報を得る方法はいくつかあります。
examples.translation: Even without the internet, there are several ways to obtain information.
examples.translationEn: Even without the internet, there are several ways to obtain information.
examples.romaji: Intānetto ga nakutomo, jōhō o eru hōhō wa ikutsu ka arimasu.

examples.sentence: 彼が困っていると聞いたら、助けずにはおかない。
examples.translation: If I hear he is in trouble, I cannot help but assist him.
examples.translationEn: If I hear he is in trouble, I cannot help but assist him.
examples.romaji: Kare ga komatte iru to kiitara, tasukezuni wa okanai.

examples.sentence: 食事の準備がこのままだと、手伝わずにはおかないだろう。
examples.translation: If the meal prep stays like this, I won't be able to stop myself from helping.
examples.translationEn: If the meal prep stays like this, I won't be able to stop myself from helping.
examples.romaji: Shokuji no junbi ga kono mama da to, tetsudawazuni wa okanai darō.

examples.sentence: この問題が解決しないなら、改善策を考えずにはおかない。
examples.translation: If this issue isn't resolved, I can't help but think of a solution.
examples.translationEn: If this issue isn't resolved, I can't help but think of a solution.
examples.romaji: Kono mondai ga kaiketsu shinai nara, kaizen-saku o kangaezuni wa okanai.

examples.sentence: 彼女が泣いているのを見たら、慰めずにはおかない。
examples.translation: When I see her crying, I cannot help but comfort her.
examples.translationEn: When I see her crying, I cannot help but comfort her.
examples.romaji: Kanojo ga naite iru no o mitara, nagusamezuni wa okanai.
